# Phantasy Documentation

> Verified docs for the flagship companion runtime

Build an AI companion or VTuber who can run her own site, content, automations, and business.

## Getting Started

- [Introduction](/docs/getting-started/introduction): Phantasy lets you build an AI companion or VTuber who can run her own site, content, automations, and business.
- [Use Cases](/docs/getting-started/use-cases): Phantasy is broad. The docs are opinionated about where it lands best.
- [Installation](/docs/getting-started/installation): Phantasy is Bun-first in the repo and npm-friendly when you install the published package.
- [First Run](/docs/getting-started/first-run): This is the quickest honest demo of Phantasy.
- [Local Development](/docs/getting-started/local-development): Phantasy is Bun-first in the main product repo and ships two common local paths: a fast repo loop and a production-like Docker loop.
- [Configuration](/docs/getting-started/configuration): Phantasy merges configuration from a few places. The order matters.
- [Bootstrapping](/docs/getting-started/bootstrapping): Phantasy has two filesystem stories:

## Product

- [Workspaces](/docs/workspaces): Every first-party Phantasy feature belongs to one primary workspace.
- [Avatar System](/docs/avatars): Phantasy supports four avatar types:
- [Website Modes](/docs/website-mode): Phantasy keeps content, themes, and public APIs in one system, but lets you choose how much of the built-in website is exposed at runtime.
- [Chronicle](/docs/features/chronicle): Chronicle is Phantasy's publishing surface.
- [Integration Model](/docs/integrations/integrations-guide): Phantasy has five extension surfaces.
- [Workflow Overview](/docs/workflows/overview): Workflows are declarative automation graphs executed by the scheduling engine.

## Guides

- [10 Minute Launch](/docs/guides/BUSINESS_AGENT_CMS_10_MINUTES): This is the shortest honest demo of Phantasy's flagship shape: a companion who runs her own site, content, automations, and business from one runtime.
- [Live2D Setup](/docs/guides/LIVE2D_SETUP): Live2D in Phantasy is intentionally pinned.
- [Authentication Setup](/docs/guides/AUTHENTICATION_SETUP): Most installs only need one thing: protect `/admin` with a local admin login and a real JWT secret.
- [Deploy](/docs/guides/DEPLOY): The primary self-hosting path is Docker.
- [Consuming Phantasy](/docs/guides/CONSUMING_PHANTASY): Build an AI companion/VTuber, then layer site, content, automations, and business around her on the same foundation.
- [Launch Playbook](/docs/guides/LAUNCH_PLAYBOOK): This is the launch filter for Phantasy.

## Architecture

- [Design Principles](/docs/architecture/design-principles): Phantasy lets you build an AI companion/VTuber who can run her own site, content, automations, and business.
- [Runtime Packages](/docs/architecture/runtime-packages): Phantasy exposes one default package plus focused runtime surfaces for advanced composition.
- [System Design](/docs/architecture/system-design): Phantasy has two runtime entrypoints that share one core:
- [Memory System](/docs/architecture/memory-system): Phantasy is markdown-first by default.
- [Agent Compatibility](/docs/architecture/agent-compatibility): Phantasy intentionally supports the common local-agent surfaces people already use across modern coding-agent stacks:
- [Heartbeat](/docs/architecture/heartbeat): Heartbeat is the lightweight background loop.
- [Admin UI Style Policy](/docs/architecture/admin-ui-style-policy): This is the style contract for Phantasy's admin UI.
- [Repository Standards](/docs/architecture/repo-standards): `bun run validate:standards` is the repo-specific guardrail layer.

## Extensions

- [Plugin Overview](/docs/plugins/overview): Phantasy uses a code-first plugin framework built around:
- [Developing Plugins](/docs/plugins/developing): Build a plugin when profiles, skills, and MCP are not enough.

## API & Reference

- [CLI](/docs/cli): The CLI is the coder-first path through Phantasy.
- [Public API](/docs/api/public-api): Phantasy exposes a small public HTTP surface alongside the authenticated admin shell.
- [Admin API](/docs/api/admin-api): The admin surface is mounted under `/admin/api/*` and is protected by the same auth system as the admin UI.

## Generated Inventories

- [Defaults](/docs/generated/defaults): This file is generated from `src/config/constants.ts`.
- [Providers](/docs/generated/providers): This file is generated from the registered runtime provider definitions.
- [Package Exports](/docs/generated/package-exports): This file is generated from `package.json` exports.
- [Plugins](/docs/generated/plugins): This file is generated from the built-in profile descriptors.
- [Skills](/docs/generated/skills): This file is generated from bundled `skills/*/SKILL.md` files.
- [Themes](/docs/generated/themes): This file is generated from bundled `themes/*/theme.json` manifests.
- [CLI Commands](/docs/generated/cli-commands): This file is generated from the CLI entrypoint command dispatch table.
- [Admin Tabs](/docs/generated/admin-tabs): This file is generated from the shared admin tab registry used by the layout, URL resolver, and command palette.
- [Party-HQ Protocol](/docs/generated/party-hq-protocol): This file is generated from `apps/party-hq/packages/shared/src/protocol.ts`.
- [API Routes](/docs/generated/api-routes): This file is generated from mounted admin route handlers and explicit Express admin router mounts.

## Development

- [Verification](/docs/development/verification): Prefer targeted verification first, then escalate to the broader suite when the change surface warrants it.
